title: lookup_proposed_matches_static input
description: Static test input for public.lookup_proposed_matches_static — no database tables required. requestor_role mirrors lookup_proposed_matches(request_type): student → tutor candidates, tutor → student candidates when candidate.role is set.

requestor_role.description: Who is requesting a match. When set, candidates with explicit role must be the opposite type (student requestor keeps only role=tutor candidates; tutor requestor keeps role=student). Candidates without role are still included.

requestor_subjects.description: Subject tags in priority order: index 0 is most important. Matching uses public.pairing_subject_priority_alignment (weighted by positions in both lists), not unordered set overlap.

id.description: Stable key for this candidate; used by exclude_ids and returned as candidate_id.

role.description: Optional. When requestor_role is set, only the opposite role is scored; omit role to always include the candidate.

priority.description: Queue priority (lower is better). Omitted or invalid → treated as 2.

subjects.description: Candidate subject tags in priority order (first = strongest). Same pairing_subject_priority_alignment scoring as requestor_subjects.

exclude_ids.description: Candidate id values to skip (e.g. already paired). Omit or use [] for none.

limit.description: Max rows returned (same as p_limit on the SQL function).
